text.title: design-tokens-basics Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for design-tokens-basics. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Structure tokens in three levels: primitive (raw values), semantic (purpose aliases), component (per-component overrides). Semantic tokens reference primitives; component tokens reference semantics. Never skip a level or create circular references.
rule.rationale: The hierarchy decouples value from purpose. Changing a brand colour requires updating one primitive; all semantic aliases that reference it update automatically, propagating to every component token. Breaking the hierarchy creates maintenance debt.

rule.statement: Primitive token names describe what they are (color-blue-500, spacing-4). Semantic token names describe purpose (color-text-primary, spacing-content-gap). Component token names describe the component and property (button-padding-horizontal).
rule.rationale: Purpose-based semantic and component names survive redesigns. Value-based names break the abstraction the instant the underlying value changes. Use consistent prefixes across categories.

rule.statement: Use consistent naming prefixes (e.g., color-, spacing-, font-size-) across all token files. Prefix order: category.role.state.
rule.rationale: Consistent prefixes enable tooling to group and organize tokens automatically. Style Dictionary and Tokens Studio both rely on path structure for platform transforms.

rule.statement: Define each token as a nested JSON object with a value key. Use {category.scale} reference syntax for aliases. Group files by layer: primitive.json, semantic.json, semantic-dark.json, components/.
rule.rationale: Style Dictionary and Tokens Studio both consume this W3C community group format. Consistent structure enables automated multi-platform compilation and round-tripping.

rule.statement: Cover at minimum: color (full scale), spacing (0–20), fontSize (xs–4xl), fontWeight (normal/medium/semibold/bold), borderRadius (none–full), shadow (sm–xl). Add animation/duration tokens when motion is part of the design system.
rule.rationale: Incomplete token coverage forces developers to hardcode values in components, re-introducing the single-source-of-truth problem the token system was meant to solve.

rule.statement: Store token files under tokens/ at the project root, mirroring the three-level hierarchy. Component token files go in tokens/components/ named after the component.
rule.rationale: A predictable directory structure allows tooling (Style Dictionary transforms) to be configured once and applied to all tokens without per-file overrides. Teams can add new component tokens without changing build config.

rule.statement: Store all token files in Git alongside application code. Apply semantic versioning: breaking value changes = major bump; new tokens = minor; fixes = patch.
rule.rationale: Tokens in design tools only (Figma, Sketch) are inaccessible to CI/CD and drift from implementation. Version control makes token changes reviewable, diffable, and rollback-safe. Semver enables coordinated upgrades across teams.
